content: iOS Safari mobile UX pitfalls: (1) 100vh includes the browser chrome (address bar + bottom bar) — a full-height container using height:100vh overflows off-screen, hiding bottom buttons. Apple confirmed this is intentional, not a bug. Fix: use dvh (dynamic viewport height) or the JS workaround: document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--vh', window.innerHeight/100+'px') with a resize listener. Safari 15+ supports svh/dvh/lvh units. (2) Bottom sheets using position:fixed with a high z-index can still be covered by iOS Safari's bottom toolbar in some cases — use env(safe-area-inset-bottom) with padding-bottom to push content above hardware notch/home indicator. (3) Touch events: passive:true is required on touchstart/touchmove listeners to avoid scroll jank; iOS ignores event.preventDefault() on passive listeners. (4) -webkit-tap-highlight-color should be set to transparent on interactive elements to remove the grey flash on tap. (5) Keyboard appearance (virtual keyboard) resizes the viewport on iOS — use visualViewport API to detect and adjust.
